ingredients.name: Wine Shop Nakagawa Liquor Store
ingredients.localName: ワイン専門店 中川酒店
ingredients.address: Wine Shop Nakagawa Liquor Store, 331-1 Kamiozomachi, Utsunomiya, Tochigi 320-0013, Japan

ingredients.name: Kamo Ohashi Bridge
ingredients.address: Kajiicho, Kamigyo Ward, Kyoto, 602-0841, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This bridge over the Kamo River appears in the opening sequence and in episode 8, where Tamako and her friends gather by the riverbank and Mochizou watches Tamako from across the water, capturing his quiet longing for her.

ingredients.name: DELTA Bistro, Coffee Shop & Gallery by KYOTOGRAPHIE
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒602-0826 Kyoto, Kamigyo Ward, Saneicho, 62 DELTA Bistro, Coffee Shop & Gallery by KYOTOGRAPHIE

ingredients.name: Demachi Futaba
ingredients.address: Demachi Futaba, 236 Seiryucho, Kamigyo Ward, Kyoto, 602-0822, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This famous wagashi shop near the Kamogawa Delta inspired the mochi-shop setting of the anime; pilgrims visit to taste the real Kyoto confections that color Tamako's everyday shopping-street world.

ingredients.name: Fujinomori Station
ingredients.address: Fujinomori Station, Fukakusa Kitorochō, Fushimi Ward, Kyoto, 612-0025, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Keihan Fujinomori Station and its surrounding Fukakusa neighborhood serve as the primary real-world model for the shopping street district; the station exterior appears in multiple early episodes as Tamako and friends pass through on daily errands.

ingredients.name: Izumoya
ingredients.address: Pontocho Idumoya, 173-2 Kashiwayacho, Nakagyo Ward, Kyoto, 604-8014, Japan

ingredients.name: S.O.U./Violet Claire
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒602-0824 Kyoto, Kamigyo Ward, Isshincho, 65-1 2階 S.O.U./Violet&Claire

ingredients.name: Matsuya Shokuryohinten
ingredients.address: Matsuya Ginza, 3 Chome-6-1 Ginza, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-8130, Japan

ingredients.name: Kamogawa Delta
ingredients.address: Kamogawa Delta, Shimogamo Miyakawacho, Sakyo Ward, Kyoto, 606-0801, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The triangular sandbar where the Kamo and Takano rivers meet appears as a backdrop for relaxed hangout scenes; in episode 8 Tamako and her friends spend time near the water here, with the stepping stones and open sky matching the anime's warm slice-of-life tone.

ingredients.name: Semmaruya honten
ingredients.localName: 千丸屋本店 京湯葉鍋と京湯葉土産 [Senmaruya - Restaurant for Yuba Lunch in Kyoto]
ingredients.address: Semmaruya honten, 541 Yaoyacho, Nakagyo Ward, Kyoto, 604-8123, Japan

ingredients.name: Yosaku
ingredients.entityContext: The okonomiyaki restaurant near Fujinomori Station is one of the small eateries in the Fukakusa shopping street area that inspired the Usagiyama shotengai ensemble; it appears among the background storefronts in early episodes.

ingredients.name: jumbo takoyaki
ingredients.entityContext: This takoyaki stall near the Fujinomori shopping strip is among the street-food spots that shaped the anime's lively shotengai atmosphere; its exterior is visible in the background of shopping-street crowd scenes.

ingredients.name: Sushi Fujii
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒602-0825 Kyoto, Kamigyo Ward, Omotecho, Sushi Fujii, 今出川上る東入表町26

ingredients.name: Inoue Drapery Shop
ingredients.entityContext: The drapery shop in the Demachi area is documented as one of the real storefronts that inspired the Usagiyama shopping district's fabric and kimono-related shops, reflecting the traditional mercantile mix of the show's setting.

ingredients.name: Boai Bookstore
ingredients.entityContext: The bookstore near Demachi served as visual reference for the shotengai's book and stationery shops; it appears in episode 2 background shots as Tamako wanders the arcade.

ingredients.name: Horiguchi Dental Clinic
ingredients.address: Horiguchi Dental Clinic, 3 Chome-7-28-101 Nagahashi, Nishinari Ward, Osaka, 557-0025, Japan

ingredients.name: Houmei Pharmacy Fujinomori Store
ingredients.localName: ほうめい薬局 ふじのもり店
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒612-0889 Kyoto, Fushimi Ward, Fukakusasu Jikaibashi, 4 Chome−360 ほうめい薬局 ふじのもり店
ingredients.entityContext: The pharmacy near Fujinomori Station is among the Fukakusa-area shops that provided reference for the Usagiyama shotengai's everyday service storefronts; it appears in episode 3 street-scene backgrounds.

ingredients.name: Lawson Fukakusa Fujimori
ingredients.localName: ローソン 深草藤森店
ingredients.address: Lawson Fukakusa Fujimori, 4 Chome-356 Fukakusasu Jikaibashi, Fushimi Ward, Kyoto, 612-0889, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The convenience store just outside Fujinomori Station appears briefly in episode 3 as the modern counterpoint to the old-fashioned shotengai, grounding the show's Fushimi Ward setting.

ingredients.name: Fushimi Police Station Fukakusa Police Box
ingredients.address: Fushimi Police Station, 101 Shimotoba Joshungamaecho, Fushimi Ward, Kyoto, 612-8384, Japan

ingredients.name: Nomakokoro Clinic
ingredients.localName: のまこころクリニック
ingredients.entityContext: The small clinic near the Fukakusa shopping strip is one of the offgrid spots documented by pilgrims as a background reference for the neighborhood doctor's office seen in episode 3.

ingredients.name: Satoutsuki
ingredients.address: Satoutsuki, 81 Ryuhonjimaecho, Kamigyo Ward, Kyoto, 602-0831, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The izakaya near Demachi appears in episode 4 background scenes representing the kind of neighborhood drinking spot the adults of the shotengai frequent after hours.

ingredients.name: Keihan Chushojima / Fushimiko Koen
ingredients.localName: 京阪中書島・伏見港公園
ingredients.entityContext: The Chushojima station area and adjacent Fushimi Port Park appear in episode 5's group outing, with the old port canal scenery providing the backdrop for the characters' rare trip away from the shopping street.

ingredients.name: Fushimi Port Park
ingredients.address: Fushimi Port Park, Yoshijima Kanaidocho, Fushimi Ward, Kyoto, 612-8225, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: In episode 5, the shopping-street gang takes a day trip and the park's riverside promenade stands in for the excursion destination; the open waterfront scenery matches the carefree group-outing mood of that episode.

ingredients.name: Tango Yura Beach
ingredients.address: Tango Yura Beach, Yura, Miyazu, Kyoto 626-0071, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The beach at Tango Yura in northern Kyoto Prefecture appears in episode 5's sea-trip sequence, where the shopping-street kids enjoy the ocean for the first time and Dela the tropical bird dramatically reacts to the familiar seaside.

ingredients.name: Bubu Park Demachishotengaikita Coin Parking Lot
ingredients.localName: ブーブーパーク平等院蓮華1コインパーキング
ingredients.address: Bubu Park Byodoin Renge 1 Coin Parking Lot, Renge-57-1 Uji, Kyoto 611-0021, Japan

ingredients.name: Demachi Rororo
ingredients.address: Demachi Rororo, 67-1 Isshincho, Kamigyo Ward, Kyoto, 602-0824, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Kyoto-style restaurant near Demachi appears in episode 6 as a dining reference location; its understated facade and narrow entrance match the background restaurant designs in the anime.

ingredients.name: Hanafuji
ingredients.entityContext: The flower shop along the Demachi shopping lanes is a documented pilgrimage spot referenced as background scenery in episode 6; its colorful storefront echoes the flower-shop aesthetic of the Usagiyama arcade.

ingredients.name: Demachi Bridge
ingredients.address: Demachi Bridge, 231 Seiryucho, Kamigyo Ward, Kyoto, 606-0801, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Demachi Bridge over the Kamo River appears in episode 7; it is one of the recurring riverside spots where characters walk and reflect, reinforcing the show's gentle portrayal of Kyoto's Demachi neighborhood.

ingredients.name: Yebisu-ya
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒700-0821 Okayama, Kita Ward, Nakasange, 1 Chome−10−30 YEBISU YA PRO

ingredients.name: Koyama
ingredients.entityContext: The shop near the Fujinomori area is logged by pilgrims as a background reference in episode 9, part of the dense commercial streetscape that gives the Usagiyama arcade its crowded, lived-in feel.

ingredients.name: GRACE
ingredients.entityContext: This offgrid spot near Fujinomori is noted in pilgrimage records as a background location in episode 9, one of many small storefronts that collectively build the anime's shotengai atmosphere.

ingredients.name: Hirayama Tokihodo
ingredients.entityContext: The clock and sundry shop near Fujinomori is documented as a background reference for episode 9; its traditional shopfront style aligns with the eclectic mix of vendors that populate the Usagiyama arcade.

ingredients.name: Funahashiya
ingredients.address: Funahashiya, 570 Nakanocho, Shimogyo Ward, Kyoto, 600-8032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This long-established Kyoto sweets shop is documented as a visual reference for the confectionery culture surrounding the Usagiyama shotengai; it appears in pilgrim notes linked to episode 9.

ingredients.name: Mako Hair Salon
ingredients.entityContext: The hair salon near the Fujinomori shopping strip is documented in pilgrimage notes as appearing in episode 11, matching the small beauty salon that operates within the Usagiyama shotengai.

ingredients.name: Bubu Park Teramachi Imadegawakita Coin Parking Lot
ingredients.localName: ブーブーパーク(BooBooPark)寺町今出川北コインパーキング
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒602-0825 Kyoto, Kamigyo Ward, Imadegawacho, Bubu Park Teramachi Imadegawakita Coin Parking Lot, 上る表町17

ingredients.name: Yoroken Main Store
ingredients.address: Yoroken Main Store, 273-1 Shimokawabe, Kawabe, Kamo District, Gifu 509-0306, Japan

ingredients.name: Fushimi Ward
ingredients.address: Fushimi Ward, Kyoto, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The entire Fushimi Ward, particularly the Fukakusa neighborhood around Keihan Fujinomori Station, is the primary real-world district that KyoAni modeled the Usagiyama Shopping Street after; walking its streets reveals the narrow lanes and traditional shophouses that define Tamako's world.
